channel.title: BinderBrief Daily
channel.description: Daily commercial insurance and underwriting automation insights.

item.title: How Upguys turned men’s health taboos into a $25-million business
item.description:
Upguys is a Vancouver-based men’s health telemedicine company generating $25 million in annual revenue with over 50,000 active patients across six Canadian provinces. The company integrates telemedicine consultations, laboratory testing covered by public health plans, and in-house pharmacy operations to deliver prescription-based treatments, particularly focusing on testosterone replacement therapy without monthly or per-appointment fees. This vertically integrated model enhances margins by controlling dispensing and compliance, improves patient retention, and provides accessible treatment options in underserviced areas, impacting commercial healthcare delivery and pharmacy margins.
Name: Mat Rezaei, Company: Upguys, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder and pharmacist leading development of a men’s health telemedicine and pharmacy platform Name: Ramin Behzadi, Company: Upguys, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder focused on regulatory compliance and business scaling of Upguys Name: Rob Shaw, Company: BCBusiness, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Journalist and author of the article covering business and health sectors in British Columbia

item.title: The Top 15 AI Fintech, InsurTech & Compliance Scale-Ups You Need to Know in 2026
item.description:
Anzen raised $16 million in a Series A round to develop Anzen Pro, an AI-driven platform for specialty commercial insurance that restructures broker workflows by converting unstructured submissions into structured data and integrating with existing agency management systems. Avantos secured $35 million in total funding to build an AI-native financial operating system that uses a knowledge graph to unify client data across custodians, CRMs, underwriting, and policy tools, enabling AI agents to automate tasks without manual reconciliation. These platforms reduce overhead and improve operational throughput, increasing broker margins and enabling carriers and financial institutions to scale underwriting, distribution, and compliance workflows more efficiently.
Name: Max Bruner, Company: Anzen, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Founder of Anzen, a company focused on AI-powered specialty commercial insurance distribution. Name: Bassam Chaptini, Company: Avantos, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder of Avantos, AI-native financial operating system for wealth managers and insurance carriers. Name: Rabih Ramadi, Company: Avantos, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder of Avantos, specializing in data integration and AI automation for financial services. Name: Richa Kaul, Company: Complyance, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of Complyance, developing AI agents to automate risk and compliance workflows. Name: Joshua Summers, Company: EnFi, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of EnFi, delivering AI credit analysts for community banks’ commercial lending. Name: Scott Weller, Company: EnFi, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CTO of EnFi, focusing on agentic AI credit analysis automation. Name: Mike Witte, Company: Equal Parts, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO and founder of Equal Parts, a platform acquiring independent insurance agencies and applying AI to extend their reach. Name: Arjun Mangla, Company: Fulcrum, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO and co-founder of Fulcrum, AI platform replacing offshore BPO for insurance brokerages. Name: Sambhav Anand, Company: Fulcrum, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CTO and co-founder of Fulcrum, specializing in AI automation of brokerage servicing tasks. Name: Stuart Jones Jr., Company: Sigma360, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of Sigma360, unified AI platform for financial crime compliance and risk intelligence. Name: JD Ross, Company: WithCoverage, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder of WithCoverage, addressing conflict of interest in insurance brokers via AI and flat-fee models. Name: Max Brenner, Company: WithCoverage, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder of WithCoverage, leveraging AI-driven risk analysis for insurance coverage optimization. Name: Dakotah Rice, Company: Harper, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of Harper, building a fully autonomous AI-based commercial insurance brokerage. Name: Tushar Nair, Company: Harper, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CTO of Harper, developing AI systems to automate complex commercial insurance brokerage workflows. Name: Apurv Bansal, Company: Zenskar, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO and co-founder of Zenskar, AI-native platform for complex billing and revenue automation. Name: Saurabh Agrawal, Company: Zenskar, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder of Zenskar, focusing on graph-based contract and billing data automation. Name: Dominic Vitucci, Company: Onshore, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of Onshore, delivering AI automation for tax credit studies and accounting workflows. Name: Brian Roberts, Company: Andreessen Horowitz (a16z), Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Partner at Andreessen Horowitz and board member of Petual, an AI platform for SOX compliance automation.

item.title: NTT DATA: Insurance Industry's US$700bn Cyber Risk Challenge
item.description:
NTT DATA’s Insurtech Global Outlook 2026 report projects uninsured cyber losses rising from US$171bn in 2023 to over US$700bn by 2030. The report highlights insurer challenges in scaling AI solutions due to governance and trust issues despite AI delivering up to 35% cost savings and increasing demand for prevention-led services. This accelerating cyber risk and slow AI adoption pressure insurers to improve underwriting efficiency and shift toward continuous risk detection to protect margins and capacity.
Name: Bruno Abril, Company: NTT DATA, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Global Head of Insurance responsible for insurance industry strategy at NTT DATA

item.title: Cyber, Catastrophe, and AI Top Risk Concerns as Protection Gaps Persist, Survey Finds
item.description:
The State of Vermont appointed Christine Brown as Deputy Commissioner of Captive Insurance in 2026, overseeing a leading onshore captive insurance domicile with over 1,200 licensed captives. Vermont's captive division employs extensive industry expertise and is modernizing technology systems, including exploring AI tools, to improve regulatory responsiveness and efficiency. This approach supports stable regulation that sustains captive owner success, enhances market confidence, and helps maintain Vermont’s competitive position in captive insurance.
Name: Christine Brown, Company: State of Vermont, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Deputy Commissioner of Captive Insurance leading Vermont’s captive insurance division Name: Kaj Samsom, Company: State of Vermont, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Commissioner of the Department of Financial Regulation supporting the captive insurance division

item.title: Insurtech EIP launches AI-powered claims automation tool
item.description:
Insurtech company EIP has launched Virtual TPAi, an AI-powered claims automation tool that handles insurance claims processing from first notification of loss to settlement. The system uses a voice-led AI agent to conduct multi-language conversations with customers, feeding data into a configurable rules engine that applies insurer-defined criteria for automatic claim approval or human review. This technology reduces insurers’ reliance on third-party claims handlers, potentially improving underwriting efficiency and lowering operational costs.
Name: Ross Sinclair, Company: EIP, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief executive at EIP Name: David Michell Dawson, Company: EIP, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Director of product at EIP Name: Iris Hakaj, Company: Insurance Times, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Reporter at Insurance Times covering insurance sector

item.title: Insurance Leaders Convene in Seoul on AI Risk
item.description:
More than 1,300 insurance leaders from 175 companies across 27 countries attended the Korea International Insurance Conference (KIIC) 2026 in Seoul, focusing on AI-driven underwriting, climate risk, cyber insurance, and autonomous vehicles. The event featured presentations of the K-Risk Barometer framework for standardizing social and environmental hazards, insights on AI integration in underwriting and claims processing, and discussions on regulatory and data infrastructure developments for autonomous driving insurance. These developments aim to improve risk identification, underwriting precision, and operational efficiency, potentially increasing capacity and margins for carriers and brokers while addressing evolving risk landscapes.
Name: Kim Tae-yun, Company: Hanyang University, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Professor who presented the K-Risk Barometer framework for risk standardization. Name: Ha Jong-mok, Company: Ministry of the Interior and Safety, Email: N/A, Type: Government, Info: Director General outlining disaster resilience certification and its impact on insurance pricing. Name: Jeong Su-jong, Company: Seoul National University, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Professor who presented research on South Korea's insurance protection gap. Name: Kevin Russell, Company: McKinsey Global Institute, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Senior fellow who spoke on AI-driven industrial transformation. Name: Beatrix Hartinger, Company: Munich Re, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief Underwriting Officer analyzing demographic and technological changes in reinsurance. item.title: AI Risk Worries Insurers and Businesses Alike
item.description:
The insurance industry is responding to emerging AI risks by either explicitly excluding AI-related damages from traditional policies or developing new policies specifically for AI exposures, while the AI-driven cyber threat frequency is rising. Companies like Resilience and Munich Re are distinguishing AI risk from conventional cyber risks by adjusting policy language and coverage scope, using data on AI-enhanced cyberattack claims and AI operational hazards for underwriting. This differentiation and evolving policy framework affect commercial broker margins and carrier underwriting by creating new risk categories and coverage requirements, increasing the need for precise risk assessment and governance to manage capacity and liabilities.
Name: Maria Long, Company: Resilience, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief underwriting officer at Resilience, a cyber resilience and insurance provider Name: Michael von Gablenz, Company: Munich Re / HSB, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Head of the Insure AI team offering AI liability insurance for businesses Name: Gerry Glombicki, Company: Fitch Ratings, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Head of cyber risk at Fitch Ratings, responsible for analysis of AI-related risks Name: Robert Lemos, Company: Dark Reading, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Contributing writer specializing in technology and cybersecurity reporting

item.title: AI at Planners' Side... Transforming the Insurance Sales Field
item.description:
Mirae Asset Life Insurance, NH NongHyup Life Insurance, and Hanwha Life have introduced generative AI systems to support insurance sales, underwriting, and planner training. These systems analyze customer health data, policy structures, and generate consultation scripts using large language models and integrated data sources to improve planning accuracy and sales support. The adoption of AI enhances planner productivity and consultation quality while raising concerns about automation bias and dependency risks that could affect underwriting decisions and sales completeness.
Name: Lee Seunghyeong, Company: The Asia Business Daily, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the insurance AI article Name: Mirae Asset Life Insurance, Company: Mirae Asset Life Insurance, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: First insurer to launch a generative AI underwriting system using large language models Name: NH NongHyup Life Insurance, Company: NH NongHyup Life Insurance, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Developer of AI-based policy planning system optimizing insurance product designs Name: Hanwha Life, Company: Hanwha Life, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: User of generative AI system 'AI STS' for planner training and generating consultation scripts

item.title: How IAT Insurance Group is building for the age of AI
item.description:
IAT Insurance Group is entering an optimization phase after nearly a decade of rebuilding its technology infrastructure, focusing on integrating predictive modeling, AI, and data tools for underwriting and claims teams. The company uses a structured decision framework to evaluate build-versus-buy options for AI capabilities and relies on an internal governance and change management model involving all management levels and end users. This strategy enhances underwriting and claims responsiveness and precision while facing potential regulatory constraints related to AI oversight, impacting carrier operational efficiency and risk management capacity.
Name: Todd Bateson, Company: IAT Insurance Group, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Executive Vice President responsible for technology infrastructure modernization and AI strategy at IAT Insurance Group Name: Chris Davis, Company: Insurance Business, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the article covering IAT Insurance Group's technology transformation

item.title: New KYC Tool From Trustd -
item.description:
Trustd has launched Know Your Carrier (KYC), a web-based tool providing instant risk assessments on carriers via email with inputs like email address and VAT number. The tool verifies email legitimacy, matches domain and business registration data, scans for adverse media, sanctions, court judgments, and uses Trustd’s network intelligence, integrating into existing workflows without installation. This tool aims to reduce cargo theft risks linked to carrier impersonation, improving underwriting accuracy and lowering loss exposure for commercial brokers and carriers in European freight markets.
Name: Lyall Cresswell, Company: Trustd, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO and Founder of TEG and Trustd, expert in freight-matching infrastructure and digital identity verification.

item.title: How broken interactions are costing insurers growth
item.description:
Insurer Earnix identified a persistent gap between advanced back-office systems and customer-facing interactions despite widespread digital transformation investments over the past decade. This gap arises because key decision-making data—pricing logic, underwriting rules, and policy details—rarely integrates seamlessly into customer engagement channels, leading to delays and inconsistent responses during customer interactions. Closing this gap by embedding decisioning capabilities directly in customer workflows can improve underwriting efficiency, reduce operational costs, and enhance broker margins by reducing customer churn and improving retention.
Name: N/A, Company: Earnix, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: A technology provider specializing in insurance pricing and underwriting decision systems

item.title: D&O Market Expected to Tighten Under Pressure, Says AM Best
item.description:
Analysts at AM Best reported that the Directors & Officers (D&O) liability insurance market faced its fourth consecutive year of premium declines in 2025, with a direct loss ratio rising to 54.5% and reserve inadequacies noted for 2023 and 2024 accident years. The report highlights slower claims closure rates, increased underwriting risk due to geopolitical and regulatory factors, and the need for insurers to perform detailed due diligence on industry sectors while managing emerging risks such as AI disclosure and ESG compliance. These developments indicate pressure on underwriting margins, a tightening market capacity, and reduced broker flexibility in providing competitive D&O insurance products in 2026.
Name: David Blades, Company: AM Best, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Associate director at the insurance rating firm AM Best who provided analysis on the D&O liability market trends Name: Chad Hemenway, Company: Carrier Management, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the article reporting on the AM Best D&O liability market analysis

item.title: Register: Risky Future AI Tools for Retail Agents 'Demo Day' on June 10
item.description:
Insurance Journal’s Risky Future is hosting an AI Tools for Retail Agents Demo Day on June 10, featuring companies that provide AI solutions for retail insurance agencies. The event demonstrates AI applications such as workflow automation, customer service tools, cross-sell gap identification, and AMS integrations designed to improve sales, servicing, and renewal processes. These AI tools aim to increase agency efficiency, reduce manual work time, and potentially improve broker margins and carrier underwriting operations by automating routine tasks.
Name: Libertate Representative, Company: Libertate, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Develops AI customer-facing agents for carriers, agents, MGAs, and insurance operations. Name: M-Files Representative, Company: M-Files, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Provides metadata-driven document management systems to support faster business processes and AI readiness. Name: 1Fort AI Representative, Company: 1Fort AI, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Creates AI systems to reduce brokers’ manual work and increase client interaction time. Name: Ennabl Representative, Company: Ennabl, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Produces tools that automate administrative tasks by extracting and verifying data from documents and AMS systems.

item.title: The Readiness is All: New AIG CEO Andersen Talks Industry Relevance
item.description:
AIG appointed Eric Andersen as CEO on June 1, 2026, and he outlined the company’s focus on readiness for evolving risks, particularly emphasizing data center risks as a major opportunity that requires collaboration with alternative capital providers. Andersen described AIG’s use of AI to enhance underwriting and claims processes by automating data ingestion, improving risk modeling, and enabling underwriters to focus on higher-level analysis, while also educating new capital on risk parameters. This approach aims to improve underwriting efficiency and capacity, broaden risk-sharing with alternative capital, and maintain broker and insurer relevance amid technological changes.
Name: Eric Andersen, Company: AIG, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of AIG since June 2026 speaking on industry readiness and AI integration Name: Lawrence Wilkinson, Company: S&P Global Ratings, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Managing Director at S&P Global Ratings and moderator of the CEO Insights session

item.title: Cyber, AI and economic pressures dominate insurance concerns in RiskScan 2026
item.description:
RiskScan 2026 is a joint research study by the Insurance Information Institute (Triple-I) and Munich Re US based on a survey of more than 1,700 respondents, identifying cyber incidents, economic pressures, and artificial intelligence as the top insurance concerns. The study uses online survey data from U.S. and U.K. markets to analyze the convergence of cyber, economic, technological, and catastrophe risks and their systemic interconnections. These findings affect commercial underwriting by highlighting the need for updated cyber coverage, new catastrophe models, and AI-related product development, influencing insurer risk management and capacity allocation.
Name: Marcus Winter, Company: Munich Re US, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: President and CEO North America (P&C Re) at Munich Re US Name: Michel Léonard, Ph.D., CBE, Company: Insurance Information Institute (Triple-I), Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief economist and data scientist at Triple-I Name: Sean Kevelighan, Company: Insurance Information Institute (Triple-I), Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of Triple-I Name: Kerri Hamm, Company: Munich Re US, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Head of Cyber Underwriting, Client Solutions, and Business Development at Munich Re US Name: Kane Wells, Company: Reinsurance News, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the article and member of Reinsurance News team

item.title: Lemonade brings renters insurance to North Dakota
item.description:
Lemonade, a technology-driven insurance provider with over three million active customers, has launched its renters insurance product in North Dakota. The product operates through Lemonade’s app, enabling users to obtain quotes, purchase, update policies, and file claims, with about 40% of claims resolved instantly. This expansion offers North Dakota renters access to lower-priced renters insurance, which at about 30% below the national average, could pressure traditional carriers’ market share and enhance underwriting efficiency through digital automation.
Name: Dan Timsit, Company: Lemonade, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Head of renters insurance at Lemonade who commented on the company’s expansion into North Dakota

item.title: How Digit is uing AI-led claims engine to speed up insurance settlements
item.description:
Digit Insurance has implemented an AI-led, event-driven claims processing system that achieves near real-time settlements with specific performance metrics such as 60% of health insurance cashless hospitalizations approved within 20 minutes and 71% of motor repair approvals processed within 12 hours. The system automates document intake, classification, validation, and parallel decision-making steps using cloud-native technology and machine learning confidence models that automatically approve straightforward claims while routing complex cases to human reviewers. This approach improves operational efficiency, reduces manual workloads, enhances underwriting accuracy, and supports faster settlements that positively impact customer retention and claims leakage reduction.
Name: Vishal Shah, Company: Digit Insurance, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Head of Data Science at Digit Insurance responsible for the AI-led claims architecture and automation strategy

item.title: Specialty Insurance Market Expected to Reach $361.64 Billion by 2035
item.description:
The global specialty insurance market was valued at $110.39 billion in 2025 and is forecast to reach $361.64 billion by 2035, growing at a 12.73% CAGR. This growth is driven by increased cyber risks and climate-related catastrophes, supported by digital distribution platforms and data-driven underwriting models, including recent AI-native underwriting tools integrating cloud platforms. The expansion affects broker distribution share, with brokers controlling 58.61% currently, while insurtech and digital channels grow rapidly, improving underwriting speed and broadening capacity for emerging risks.
Name: Lloyd’s of London, Company: Lloyd’s of London, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Global insurance and reinsurance marketplace. Name: Google Cloud, Company: Google Cloud, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Cloud computing services provider collaborating on AI-native underwriting platforms. Name: AXA XL, Company: AXA XL, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Specialty insurance provider expanding products in nature-based risk and biodiversity liability aligned with EU frameworks. Name: CNPC, Company: CNPC, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Chinese state-owned petroleum corporation contributing to Asia Pacific insurance demand. Name: CNOOC, Company: CNOOC, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Chinese state-owned oil company involved in offshore drilling influencing regional risk exposure. Name: Sinopec, Company: Sinopec, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Chinese oil and gas enterprise impacting insurance needs in the Asia Pacific region.

item.title: VisitorsCoverage Launches Treppy MCP Server to Power AI-Driven Travel Insurance
item.description:
VisitorsCoverage Inc. launched the Treppy MCP server, a standardized AI integration platform enabling real-time travel insurance quotes from multiple carriers via a single endpoint. The server supports AI agents compliant with the Anthropic MCP standard, providing up-to-date plan details and seamless quote-to-compare workflows within AI booking and concierge systems. This technology reduces integration complexity, improving underwriting efficiency and enabling brokers and carriers to serve AI-driven purchase channels with lower operational friction.
Name: Rajeev Shrivastava, Company: VisitorsCoverage Inc., Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of VisitorsCoverage Inc.

item.title: ReSource Pro Releases Original Four-Part Research Series to Help Insurance Agencies Navigate a Tech Landscape Transformed by AI
item.description:
ReSource Pro released the Agency Tech Blueprint, a four-part research series designed to help retail insurance agencies evaluate and implement technology in an AI-driven environment. The series uses a three-level maturity model categorizing agency technology into foundational, optimizing, and transformational systems to guide investment and operational improvements. This framework aims to improve broker operational efficiency and supports agencies in adopting AI to enhance underwriting and client service without increasing risk.
Name: Mark Breading, Company: ReSource Pro, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Senior Partner leading ReSource Pro Insights research and advisory. Name: Dan Epstein Tagger, Company: ReSource Pro, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of ReSource Pro discussing AI adoption challenges in insurance. Name: Jared Kreiner, Company: ReSource Pro, Email: jared_kreiner@resourcepro.com, Type: Individual, Info: Contact person for ReSource Pro news releases. Name: Su-Ting Fu, Company: ReSource Pro, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Senior Vice President, Data Strategy at ReSource Pro responsible for enterprise data and AI capability development.
